Video.summary: Learn how to make vintage inspired barrette hair accessories.
Video.title: FaveCrafts: Vintage Inspired DIY Barrette Hair Accessories

Image.alt: FaveCrafts: Vintage Inspired DIY Barrette Hair Accessories
FaveCrafts: Vintage Inspired DIY Barrette Hair Accessories

Video.summary: Collect some rocks and turn them into these adorable pet cactus rocks. These make the cutest desk friend.
Video.title: The Best Ideas For Kids: Pet Cactus Rocks

Image.alt: The Best Ideas For Kids: Pet Cactus Rocks
The Best Ideas For Kids: Pet Cactus Rocks

Video.summary: Marc teaches how to sketch up design for a new exciting project - arts & crafts table.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Sketch Up Design for an Arts & Crafts Table

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Sketch Up Design for an Arts & Crafts Table
The Wood Whisperer: Sketch Up Design for an Arts & Crafts Table

Video.title: FaveCrafts: Vintage China Flower Display

Image.alt: FaveCrafts: Vintage China Flower Display
FaveCrafts: Vintage China Flower Display

Video.summary: Join DIY expert and mom, Lynn Lilly as she welcomes creatives, chefs, moms and entrepreneurs to the Craft Box Girls Studio. In today's show, Lynn creates her very own herb garden!
Video.title: Creative Living with Lynn Lilly: Gardening and More

Image.alt: Creative Living with Lynn Lilly: Gardening and More
Creative Living with Lynn Lilly: Gardening and More

Video.summary: Washi Tape Cards with The Crafty Chica
Video.title: The Crafty Chica: Washi Tape Cards

Image.alt: The Crafty Chica: Washi Tape Cards
The Crafty Chica: Washi Tape Cards

Video.summary: Chinda teaches how to do the Milanese lace pattern in this Stitch Along video.
Video.title: Knitting Stitch Along: Milanese Lace

Image.alt: Knitting Stitch Along: Milanese Lace
Knitting Stitch Along: Milanese Lace

Video.summary: I don't know if this ever happens to you, but sometimes my tools just get out of whack and start to sound funny. Take a listen and let me know if you have any advice. Also have you ever wondered why end grain cutting boards are prone to splitting? Here's a couple of reasons why!
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Why Did My Cutting Board Split?

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Why Did My Cutting Board Split?
The Wood Whisperer: Why Did My Cutting Board Split?

Video.summary: Learn how to properly mill your lumber with Marc in this episode because you not gonna get far without it!
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Milling Lumber aka The Jointer's Jumpin'

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Milling Lumber aka The Jointer's Jumpin'
The Wood Whisperer: Milling Lumber aka The Jointer's Jumpin'

Video.summary: I spend a lot of time helping new woodworkers solve problems. This gives me a unique perspective on the potential stumble point that most woodworkers will experience. Eventually, these things become woodworking common sense but until that time, I consider it woodworking not-so-common sense. So here are five tips that most woodworkers eventually figure out on their own.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Woodworking Not-So-Common Sense

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Woodworking Not-So-Common Sense
The Wood Whisperer: Woodworking Not-So-Common Sense

Video.summary: I sat down to design a new dining table for my brother-in-law Jason and before I was done I realized it was totally inspired by my time with Jory Brigham. The table has splayed legs, a large overhanging top, and bevels and tapers galore.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Mid-Century Modern Dining Table

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Mid-Century Modern Dining Table
The Wood Whisperer: Mid-Century Modern Dining Table

Video.summary: Back in the early days of my woodworking, I was heavily influenced by a show called Woodworks. The host, David Marks, would frequently engage in a process that was very new to me at the time: power-sculpting (aka power carving). So what you see in this video is largely inspired by the work of David Marks.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Power-Sculpted Contemplation Bench

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Power-Sculpted Contemplation Bench
The Wood Whisperer: Power-Sculpted Contemplation Bench

Video.summary: A deep red mahogany finish doesn’t have to be the result of a complicated process. I’ll show you how I use a simple two-step method to revitalize my mother in law’s old beat up rocking char. First, a coat of General Finishes Merlot Water-Based Dye. Second, a coat of General Finishes Brown Mahogany Gel Stain. Its all finished off with a nice satin lacquer topcoat.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: A Deep Red Mahogany Finish

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: A Deep Red Mahogany Finish
The Wood Whisperer: A Deep Red Mahogany Finish

Video.summary: I decided to do a little experiment for myself. I wanted to see if I could find any evidence of a weakened bond between polyurethane and waxed shellac, when the shellac is used in a typical manner as a sealer.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Shellac Under Polyurethane

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Shellac Under Polyurethane
The Wood Whisperer: Shellac Under Polyurethane

Video.summary: The best way to install knife hinges.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: How to Install Knife Hinges

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: How to Install Knife Hinges
The Wood Whisperer: How to Install Knife Hinges

Video.summary: A French cleat is as ingenious as it is simple. It involves securing a strip of wood with a 45 degree bevel to the wall, and then securing an opposing beveled strip on the back of a cabinet or anything you want to hang. Its incredibly strong and versatile. Its a great way to hang cabinetry and as you’ll see in this video, it can be used to make an awesome modular wall storage unit.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: French Cleat Storage System

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: French Cleat Storage System
The Wood Whisperer: French Cleat Storage System

Video.summary: How to make loose mortise and tenon joints with a router.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: But I Don’t Have A Domino – Loose Mortise and Tenon Joints

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: But I Don’t Have A Domino – Loose Mortise and Tenon Joints
The Wood Whisperer: But I Don’t Have A Domino – Loose Mortise and Tenon Joints

Video.summary: We made it to Missouri and I'm investigating a new rust-preventative product.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: We Moved - It’s Humid - Game Changer for Rust Prevention?!

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: We Moved - It’s Humid - Game Changer for Rust Prevention?!
The Wood Whisperer: We Moved - It’s Humid - Game Changer for Rust Prevention?!

Video.summary: A wall-mounted rack to store your 3D filament.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Filament Rack - For 3D Printing

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Filament Rack - For 3D Printing
The Wood Whisperer: Filament Rack - For 3D Printing

Video.summary: You don't need breadboard ends - and that's why.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: You Don't Need Breadboard Ends - Ask TWW

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: You Don't Need Breadboard Ends - Ask TWW
The Wood Whisperer: You Don't Need Breadboard Ends - Ask TWW

Video.summary: Not sure what woodworking clamps to buy? In this episode, Marc will answer all your questions and guide you to the best clamps!
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Guild to Woodworking Clamps aka The Big Squeeze

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Guild to Woodworking Clamps aka The Big Squeeze
The Wood Whisperer: Guild to Woodworking Clamps aka The Big Squeeze

Video.summary: I can say without a doubt that this is the best way to set up a bandsaw.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: The Best Way to Set Up a Bandsaw!

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: The Best Way to Set Up a Bandsaw!
The Wood Whisperer: The Best Way to Set Up a Bandsaw!

Video.summary: An over-designed shoe rack made with expensive wood.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Just a Shoe Rack

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Just a Shoe Rack
The Wood Whisperer: Just a Shoe Rack

Video.summary: Whenever I make legs for a table or chair, I find it difficult to leave them square and flat on each face. Ever since I saw David Marks pillow the outside faces of his Bubinga Coffee Table on WoodWorks, I always like to consider the possibility of using that technique to spice things up.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: How to Create Pillowed Legs

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: How to Create Pillowed Legs
The Wood Whisperer: How to Create Pillowed Legs

Video.summary: If you are one of those folks who wants to begin detaching from the tablesaw, you're going to need to find alternative methods for doing the things that we typically use the tablesaw for, such as cutting tenons. Fortunately, the bandsaw is a very good alternative.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Tenons on the Bandsaw

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Tenons on the Bandsaw
The Wood Whisperer: Tenons on the Bandsaw

Video.summary: While building my 6-panel cabinet recently, I decided to capture the process of mortising for a butt hinge. With a router, a small straight bit, a chisel, and some careful layout, you’ll find mortising for hinges to be fun and easy.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Butt Hinge Mortise

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Butt Hinge Mortise
The Wood Whisperer: Butt Hinge Mortise

Video.summary: A wall-hanging cabinet to help you organize and store your drill and router bits.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Bunk Bed With Slide

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Bunk Bed With Slide
The Wood Whisperer: Bunk Bed With Slide

Video.summary: A few bit types to buy and a few to avoid.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Buy This Not That - Drill Bits

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Buy This Not That - Drill Bits
The Wood Whisperer: Buy This Not That - Drill Bits

Video.summary: A simple jig that will help you clamp large miter joints, such as those found in Mid-Century Modern furniture.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Cool Miter Clamping Trick

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Cool Miter Clamping Trick
The Wood Whisperer: Cool Miter Clamping Trick

Video.summary: Marc teaches how to pop out the pattern of grains, and in particular this Maple piece!
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Popping the Grain in Maple

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Popping the Grain in Maple
The Wood Whisperer: Popping the Grain in Maple

Video.summary: I'll be the first to admit that I have a slight problem: I'm a little paranoid about dust. I want as little of the stuff in the shop air as possible so I continually seek out means to achieve that end. While I do wear a respirator while I work, I frequently have to talk on camera shortly after making cuts that put dust directly into the air.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Air Quality Upgrades

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Air Quality Upgrades
The Wood Whisperer: Air Quality Upgrades

Video.summary: Learn with Marc about the most important and the strongest joints in woodworking - Mortise and Tenon joints.
Video.title: The Wood Whisperer: Mortise and Tenon Options aka Tenons Anyone?

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Mortise and Tenon Options aka Tenons Anyone?
The Wood Whisperer: Mortise and Tenon Options aka Tenons Anyone?

Video.summary: The bed comes to life with the completion of the hardware and the support system!

Image.alt: The Wood Whisperer: Platform Bed - Hardware - Supports - Finish
The Wood Whisperer: Platform Bed - Hardware - Supports - Finish

Video.summary: Time for another frog food today we’re gonna turn my Dog Bowie into an anime character with a pop punk style. From inspo to highlights we got it covered. So sit back, relax, and enjoy!
